articles.headline: India's Economic Resilience Shines in 2024 Year-End Review, FY25 Growth Projected Strong
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Finance releases year-end economic review highlighting robust growth.
• Key drivers include domestic demand, public capital expenditure, and manufacturing.
• Inflation remains within RBI's target band, though global uncertainties persist.
• Government reiterates commitment to fiscal consolidation path for FY25.
• India poised to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: India's projected GDP growth for FY25.
Key sectors contributing to growth (e.g., manufacturing, services, agriculture).
RBI's inflation target range.
Components of fiscal deficit (revenue deficit, capital expenditure).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India's economy in the coming fiscal year.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government's fiscal and monetary policies in maintaining stability and growth.
Examine the role of domestic demand and public investment in economic recovery and expansion.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing India's GDP growth trajectory and key economic indicators.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a USD 5 trillion economy by 2027 and a USD 10 trillion economy by 2035.

articles.headline: 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' Phase II Launched to Ensure Scheme Saturation
articles.explainer:
• Government initiates second phase of the nationwide outreach program.
• Focus on ensuring 100% saturation of flagship schemes at the grassroots level.
• Aims to reach unreached beneficiaries for schemes like PM Ujjwala, Ayushman Bharat, PM-KISAN.
• Utilizes mobile vans and interactive sessions to spread awareness and facilitate enrollment.
• Emphasizes citizen participation and feedback for effective governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
prelimsPoints: Objective of Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra.
Key flagship schemes covered under the initiative (e.g., PM Ujjwala, Ayushman Bharat).
Methodology used for outreach (e.g., mobile vans, IEC activities).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of outreach programs like Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra in achieving scheme saturation and improving governance.
Discuss the challenges in last-mile delivery of welfare schemes and how such initiatives address them.
Analyze the role of technology and public participation in enhancing the impact of government welfare programs.
Examine the concept of 'Viksit Bharat' and the role of social welfare in achieving it.
articles.imageDescription: Mobile van with 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' branding reaching out to rural communities.
articles.trivia: The first phase of Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra covered over 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats across India.

articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Conclude with Breakthrough on Key Chapters, Historic Deal Expected
articles.explainer:
• After years of deliberations, India and the European Union reach consensus on critical aspects of the Free Trade Agreement.
• Breakthrough reported on Intellectual Property Rights (IPR), sustainable development, and market access for goods.
• The agreement aims to boost bilateral trade and investment significantly.
• Expected to open new avenues for Indian industries in EU markets and attract European investment.
• Formal signing anticipated in early 2025, marking a strategic partnership milestone.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Key areas of agreement in India-EU FTA (e.g., IPR, goods, services, investment).
Significance of EU as India's trading partner.
Objectives of an FTA.
Impact on specific Indian sectors (e.g., textiles, agriculture, IT).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic implications of the India-EU Free Trade Agreement for both parties.
Discuss the challenges faced during the FTA negotiations and how they were resolved.
Examine how this agreement can enhance India's economic integration into global supply chains and boost manufacturing.
Evaluate the potential impact on India's 'Make in India' initiative and export competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, and India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieves a major milestone in RLV development.
• The test involved a precision landing and autonomous navigation demonstration.
• Aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches and enhance mission flexibility.
• RLV technology is crucial for future space exploration and commercial space missions.
• Positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable launch capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space research
prelimsPoints: Full form of RLV.
Objectives of RLV technology.
Benefits of reusable launch vehicles.
Other countries developing similar technology.
ISRO's previous RLV-TD missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how RLV capabilities can enhance India's competitiveness in the global commercial space market.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for ISRO in developing fully operational RLVs.
Highlight the role of indigenization in India's space technology advancements.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) during re-entry.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD 'HEX' mission by ISRO in 2016 successfully demonstrated the hypersonic flight and autonomous landing technology.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Coastal Zone Projects, Mandates Stricter Safeguards
articles.explainer:
• Apex court rules on a batch of petitions challenging coastal development projects.
• Affirms the necessity of balancing development with environmental protection.
• Directs a review mechanism for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) in ecologically sensitive areas.
• Emphasizes the need for robust public consultation and compensation frameworks.
• Ruling provides clarity for infrastructure projects in Coastal Regulation Zones (CRZ).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of CRZ Notification.
Role of EIA in environmental clearances.
Importance of public hearing in EIA process.
Different categories of CRZ areas.
Key principles of sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's approach to balancing developmental needs with environmental protection, particularly in coastal areas.
Discuss the effectiveness of the existing Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification and suggest potential reforms.
Examine the role of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) in ensuring sustainable infrastructure development.
Evaluate the implications of this ruling for future coastal development projects and local communities.
articles.imageDescription: A developed coastal area with emphasis on both infrastructure and natural ecosystem.
articles.trivia: The CRZ Notification was first issued in 1991 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, and has been amended several times since.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 200 GW Mark, Accelerating Net-Zero Goals
articles.explainer:
• India achieves a significant milestone in renewable energy, surpassing 200 GW installed capacity.
• This includes solar, wind, hydro, and biomass power sources.
• Reflects government's aggressive push towards cleaner energy and reduced carbon footprint.
• Boosts India's commitment to its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Paves the way for greater energy security and a green economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: India's total renewable energy capacity in GW.
Breakdown of capacity by source (solar, wind, hydro).
India's target for renewable energy by 2030.
Concept of NDCs under Paris Agreement.
Government schemes promoting renewable energy (e.g., PM-KUSUM).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity and its implications for climate change mitigation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and net-zero goals.
Examine the role of government policies, private investment, and technological advancements in the green energy transition.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of shifting towards a renewable energy-dominated grid.
articles.imageDescription: Large solar panel farm stretching into the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy growth.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world, after China and the USA.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework to Combat Rising Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• Government unveils new measures to bolster national cyber defenses.
• Focuses on critical infrastructure protection and data privacy.
• Enhances cooperation between public and private sectors for threat intelligence sharing.
• Introduces updated guidelines for reporting cyber incidents and ensuring quick response.
• Aims to safeguard digital economy and citizen data from sophisticated cyber attacks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics & IT / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Role of CERT-In.
Key provisions of the IT Act, 2000 (as amended).
Importance of protecting critical information infrastructure.
Common types of cyber threats.
Concept of data localization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of India's current cyber security framework and suggest measures for further strengthening it.
Examine the challenges in fostering public-private partnership for cyber defense and data protection.
Evaluate the balance between national security concerns and individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized digital lock icon overlaid with binary code, representing cyber security.
articles.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.

articles.headline: Government Releases Draft National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Progress Report
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Education publishes a comprehensive report on the progress of NEP 2020 implementation.
• Highlights advancements in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and digital education initiatives.
• Identifies key challenges in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy.
• Invites public feedback on the report to refine future implementation strategies.
• Underscores the long-term vision of transforming India's education landscape for a knowledge economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
articles.source: Ministry of Education / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020.
Major recommendations of NEP (e.g., 5+3+3+4 structure, multidisciplinary approach).
Objectives of Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) Mission.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
Key bodies involved in NEP implementation (NCERT, UGC, AICTE).
mainsPoints: Assess the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020 since its inception.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of equity, access, and quality in Indian education.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the transformational goals of NEP.
Suggest further measures to accelerate the successful implementation of NEP across all levels of education.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Discuss Global Economic Stability and Debt Management at Year-End Meeting
articles.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors convene for their final meeting of the year.
• Deliberations focus on addressing rising global debt vulnerabilities and inflationary pressures.
• Emphasize coordinated efforts to foster sustainable and inclusive economic growth worldwide.
• India reiterates its call for reforms in multilateral development banks to address developing nations' needs.
• Joint statement highlights commitment to financial stability and resilience against future shocks.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Mandate of G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Role of G20 in global economic governance.
Current global economic challenges discussed.
India's stance on MDB reforms.
Concept of sustainable and inclusive growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic challenges such as debt distress and inflation.
Discuss India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly concerning multilateral development bank reforms.
Examine the importance of international cooperation in achieving global financial stability and sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of G20 declarations in translating into concrete policy actions by member states.
articles.imageDescription: Representatives from various countries seated around a conference table with G20 logo.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 80% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Urban Development Ministry Launches 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0' with Enhanced Focus on Sustainability and Inclusivity
articles.explainer:
• Second phase of the flagship urban development program is unveiled.
• Expands scope to include more cities and integrates lessons from the first phase.
• Prioritizes green urban mobility, digital governance, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Aims to foster citizen-centric urban planning and improve quality of life.
• Emphasizes sustainable financial models and private sector participation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Smart Cities Mission.
Key features of 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0'.
Components of a smart city (e.g., smart waste management, smart transport).
Challenges of urbanization in India.
Role of technology in urban governance.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the achievements and shortcomings of the first phase of the Smart Cities Mission in India.
Discuss how 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0' addresses the challenges of rapid urbanization and promotes sustainable urban development.
Examine the importance of citizen participation and digital governance in creating truly 'smart' and livable cities.
Suggest innovative approaches for financing and implementing urban infrastructure projects under the mission.
articles.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces and integrated technology, representing a smart city.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on 25 June 2015.

articles.headline: Agricultural Sector Registers Record Foodgrain Production in 2024, Focus Shifts to Crop Diversification
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Agriculture releases final estimates for Kharif and Rabi seasons, showing record output.
• Attributed to favorable monsoon, government support, and improved farming practices.
• Government now emphasizes crop diversification to reduce reliance on water-intensive crops.
• Promotes pulses, oilseeds, and high-value horticulture to enhance farmer incomes.
• Addresses concerns over groundwater depletion and nutritional security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / Livemint
prelimsPoints: Major foodgrain crops in India.
Difference between Kharif and Rabi crops.
Importance of monsoon for Indian agriculture.
Benefits of crop diversification.
Government initiatives for farmer welfare (e.g., PM-KISAN, FPOs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to record foodgrain production in India and its implications for food security.
Discuss the necessity and challenges of crop diversification in the context of climate change and water scarcity.
Examine the government's role in promoting sustainable agricultural practices and enhancing farmer incomes.
Evaluate the impact of agricultural policies on cropping patterns and environmental sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green agricultural fields under a clear sky, symbolizing bountiful harvest.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, and several other major foodgrains.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Global Health Diplomacy with New Vaccine Initiatives and Collaborative Research
articles.explainer:
• India expands its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative, providing assistance to developing nations.
• Announces new partnerships for vaccine research and development with international bodies.
• Focuses on equitable access to healthcare and pandemic preparedness globally.
• Highlights India's role as a reliable pharmaceutical and vaccine manufacturing hub.
• Aims to contribute significantly to global health security and universal health coverage.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Concept of Vaccine Maitri.
India's role as 'pharmacy of the world'.
Importance of vaccine equity.
International organizations involved in global health (WHO, GAVI).
India's contributions to COVAX.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing role in global health diplomacy and its contributions to addressing global health challenges.
Analyze the 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its impact on India's soft power and international relations.
Examine the challenges in ensuring equitable access to vaccines and essential medicines worldwide.
Evaluate the potential for India to become a global leader in pharmaceutical research and development, beyond manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: A map of the world with syringes and vaccine vials, symbolizing global health efforts.
articles.trivia: India is the largest provider of generic drugs globally, meeting over 50% of vaccine demand worldwide.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Electoral Funding
articles.explainer:
• Committee submits report suggesting greater transparency and accountability in political financing.
• Proposes caps on anonymous donations and real-time disclosure of contributions.
• Advocates for state funding of elections as a long-term measure to curb corruption.
• Recommends strengthening the Election Commission's powers to regulate political parties' finances.
• Aims to foster a level playing field and enhance democratic integrity.
gsSubTopics: Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
articles.source: PRS Legislative Research / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: Current mechanisms for political funding in India (e.g., electoral bonds).
Arguments for and against state funding of elections.
Powers of Election Commission of India regarding political parties.
Recommendations for increasing transparency.
Role of the Representation of the People Act.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the existing framework of electoral funding in India and its impact on democratic processes.
Discuss the various recommendations made by the Parliamentary Standing Committee for comprehensive electoral finance reforms.
Examine the pros and cons of state funding of elections as a means to curb corruption and promote transparency.
Suggest measures to strengthen the Election Commission's authority in regulating political party finances and ensuring fair elections.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing the intersection of elections and money.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a permanent and independent body established by the Constitution of India.

articles.headline: Monsoon 2024 Review: Mixed Impact on Agriculture and Water Resources, Policy Adjustments Eyed
articles.explainer:
• India Meteorological Department (IMD) releases post-monsoon assessment.
• Highlights regional variations in rainfall, with some areas facing deficit and others surplus.
• Assesses the impact on Kharif crop yields and reservoir levels across the country.
• Prompts government to review water management strategies and agricultural contingency plans.
• Emphasizes the need for climate-resilient agriculture and improved irrigation infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce
prelimsPoints: Factors influencing Indian monsoon.
Impact of El Niño/La Niña on monsoon.
Importance of monsoon for Indian economy.
Measures for drought and flood management.
Different types of irrigation systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the regional variations in Monsoon 2024 rainfall and its differential impact on India's agricultural sector and water resources.
Discuss the challenges posed by climate change on monsoon patterns and the necessity of climate-resilient agricultural strategies.
Examine the government's role in developing robust water management policies and improving irrigation infrastructure.
Suggest policy adjustments to mitigate the adverse effects of monsoon variability on farmer livelihoods and food security.
articles.imageDescription: Rain-fed agricultural fields in a rural landscape, depicting monsoon's influence.
articles.trivia: The Indian monsoon contributes to about 70% of India's annual rainfall and is crucial for agriculture.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Issues New Guidelines for Protection and Preservation of Archaeological Sites
articles.explainer:
• Comprehensive guidelines released to enhance conservation efforts for India's rich heritage.
• Focuses on integrating local communities in protection and management of sites.
• Introduces advanced digital mapping and documentation techniques for archaeological assets.
• Aims to combat illegal excavations, encroachments, and illicit trafficking of artifacts.
• Reinforces India's commitment to safeguarding its cultural identity and promoting heritage tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation of heritage sites
articles.source: Ministry of Culture / Archaeological Survey of India
keyTerms: Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act
prelimsPoints: Role of Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
Key provisions of the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act.
Importance of community involvement in heritage conservation.
Examples of World Heritage Sites in India.
Threats to archaeological sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in protecting and preserving India's vast archaeological heritage amidst developmental pressures and neglect.
Analyze the significance of community participation and advanced technology in effective site management and conservation.
Evaluate the economic potential of heritage tourism and how it can be leveraged for sustainable development.
Examine the legal and institutional framework for heritage protection in India and suggest improvements.
articles.imageDescription: Ancient ruins being carefully excavated and preserved by archaeologists.
articles.trivia: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including cultural and natural properties.

highlights: India's economy demonstrates strong resilience in 2024 year-end review, with robust growth projected for FY25.
Government launches Phase II of 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' to achieve 100% saturation of flagship welfare schemes.
Historic breakthrough reported in India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations, signaling a major boost to bilateral trade.
ISRO successfully tests next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, advancing India's space capabilities.
India's renewable energy capacity crosses 200 GW, accelerating progress towards national climate and net-zero goals.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It is an initiative aimed at promoting financial literacy among rural women.
2. It primarily focuses on ensuring 100% saturation of flagship government schemes.
3. Mobile vans are used as a key tool for outreach and beneficiary enrollment.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While financial literacy might be a component of some schemes, the primary aim of Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra is to ensure saturation of various flagship government welfare schemes. Statement 2 is correct, as the core objective is 100% saturation of schemes. Statement 3 is also correct, as mobile vans and interactive sessions are key methods for outreach and facilitating enrollment.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology in space missions?
options: (a) To increase the payload capacity for deep-space missions.
(b) To significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
(c) To enable faster travel to other planets.
(d) To facilitate human habitation on the Moon.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology's primary objective is to make space access more affordable by allowing expensive components of launch vehicles to be reused, much like airplanes. While it might indirectly support other advancements, cost reduction is the direct and most significant benefit.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about India's Renewable Energy sector:
1. India's renewable energy capacity includes solar, wind, hydro, and biomass power.
2. The country has a target to achieve Net-Zero emissions by 2030.
3. India is the largest producer of renewable energy globally.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as India's renewable energy portfolio comprises these diverse sources. Statement 2 is incorrect; India's target for Net-Zero emissions is by 2070, not 2030. Statement 3 is incorrect; India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy, after China and the USA, not the largest globally.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following pairs is NOT correctly matched regarding agricultural terms and their descriptions?
options: (a) Kharif Crops: Sown with the onset of monsoon.
(c) Crop Diversification: Shifting from water-intensive crops to less demanding ones.
(d) MSP: Minimum Support Price, ensures a floor price for certain crops.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (a) is correct; Kharif crops like rice, maize, and jowar are sown during the monsoon. Statement (b) is incorrect; Rabi crops like wheat, barley, and mustard are sown in winter (October-December) and harvested in spring (April-June), not harvested in winter. Statement (c) is correct, it's a key strategy for sustainable agriculture. Statement (d) is correct, MSP is a government intervention to protect farmers.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to India's cyber security framework, consider the following statements:
1. CERT-In is the national agency responsible for responding to cyber security incidents.
2. The framework primarily focuses on protecting critical information infrastructure.
3. Data localization mandates that all personal data of Indian citizens must be stored exclusively within India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is indeed the nodal agency. Statement 2 is correct, protection of critical infrastructure is a key pillar. Statement 3 is partially correct but the definition is too absolute; while data localization often implies storing data within the country, current Indian proposals/discussions have varying degrees and exceptions, and it's not a blanket mandate for *all* personal data to be *exclusively* stored within India without any cross-border flow provisions under certain conditions. The term 'exclusively' makes it problematic. Therefore, 3 is generally considered incorrect in its strict interpretation.

mainsMocks.question: India's economic performance in 2024 has demonstrated remarkable resilience amidst global challenges. Discuss the key drivers of this growth and the structural reforms necessary to sustain this momentum towards achieving a 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Government Budgeting.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's economic resilience in 2024. Identify key drivers like domestic demand, public investment, manufacturing push, and stable inflation. Discuss structural reforms needed: labor market, land reforms, ease of doing business, human capital development, green transition, and fiscal prudence. Conclude with a vision for 'Viksit Bharat'.

mainsMocks.question: The successful conclusion of India-EU FTA negotiations marks a significant milestone in India's economic diplomacy. Analyze the strategic and economic implications of this agreement for India, and discuss the challenges that need to be addressed for its effective implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-EU FTA as a major diplomatic achievement. Analyze strategic implications (geopolitical alignment, diversified trade partners, soft power) and economic implications (market access, investment, technology transfer, boost to specific sectors). Discuss implementation challenges like non-tariff barriers, standards harmonization, domestic industry protection, and dispute resolution mechanisms. Conclude with the potential for deeper strategic partnership.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress in renewable energy adoption, India faces challenges in achieving its ambitious climate targets. Critically examine the obstacles to a complete green energy transition in India and suggest comprehensive policy measures to overcome them.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's progress in renewable energy and its ambitious targets (e.g., 500GW by 2030, Net-Zero by 2070). Identify obstacles: grid integration, storage solutions, financing, land acquisition, supply chain vulnerabilities, coal dependency, and just transition for fossil fuel workers. Suggest policy measures: R&D in storage, smart grid infrastructure, innovative financing models, clear land policies, international collaboration, skill development, and carbon pricing.

quickBytes.text: India's target for Net-Zero emissions is by the year 2070.
The 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' aims for 100% saturation of flagship government schemes.
ISRO's RLV technology aims to reduce space launch costs and enhance mission flexibility.
The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, making the FTA crucial for economic ties.
The Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification falls under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
India is the world's largest provider of generic drugs and a major vaccine manufacturer.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 education policy.
CERT-In is India's national agency for cyber security incident response.
The G20 accounts for 80% of global GDP and 75% of global trade.
India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, covering both cultural and natural heritage.

• Reinforces India's commitment to safeguarding its cultural identity and promoting heritage tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation of heritage sites
GS1.source: Ministry of Culture / Archaeological Survey of India
keyTerms: Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act
prelimsPoints: Role of Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
Key provisions of the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act.
Importance of community involvement in heritage conservation.
Examples of World Heritage Sites in India.
Threats to archaeological sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in protecting and preserving India's vast archaeological heritage amidst developmental pressures and neglect.
Analyze the significance of community participation and advanced technology in effective site management and conservation.
Evaluate the economic potential of heritage tourism and how it can be leveraged for sustainable development.
Examine the legal and institutional framework for heritage protection in India and suggest improvements.
GS1.imageDescription: Ancient ruins being carefully excavated and preserved by archaeologists.
GS1.trivia: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including cultural and natural properties.

GS2.headline: 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' Phase II Launched to Ensure Scheme Saturation
GS2.explainer:
• Government initiates second phase of the nationwide outreach program.
• Focus on ensuring 100% saturation of flagship schemes at the grassroots level.
• Aims to reach unreached beneficiaries for schemes like PM Ujjwala, Ayushman Bharat, PM-KISAN.
• Utilizes mobile vans and interactive sessions to spread awareness and facilitate enrollment.
• Emphasizes citizen participation and feedback for effective governance.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
prelimsPoints: Objective of Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra.
Key flagship schemes covered under the initiative (e.g., PM Ujjwala, Ayushman Bharat).
Methodology used for outreach (e.g., mobile vans, IEC activities).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of outreach programs like Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra in achieving scheme saturation and improving governance.
Discuss the challenges in last-mile delivery of welfare schemes and how such initiatives address them.
Analyze the role of technology and public participation in enhancing the impact of government welfare programs.
Examine the concept of 'Viksit Bharat' and the role of social welfare in achieving it.
GS2.imageDescription: Mobile van with 'Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra' branding reaching out to rural communities.
GS2.trivia: The first phase of Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra covered over 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats across India.

GS2.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Conclude with Breakthrough on Key Chapters, Historic Deal Expected
GS2.explainer:
• After years of deliberations, India and the European Union reach consensus on critical aspects of the Free Trade Agreement.
• Breakthrough reported on Intellectual Property Rights (IPR), sustainable development, and market access for goods.
• The agreement aims to boost bilateral trade and investment significantly.
• Expected to open new avenues for Indian industries in EU markets and attract European investment.
• Formal signing anticipated in early 2025, marking a strategic partnership milestone.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Key areas of agreement in India-EU FTA (e.g., IPR, goods, services, investment).
Significance of EU as India's trading partner.
Objectives of an FTA.
Impact on specific Indian sectors (e.g., textiles, agriculture, IT).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic implications of the India-EU Free Trade Agreement for both parties.
Discuss the challenges faced during the FTA negotiations and how they were resolved.
Examine how this agreement can enhance India's economic integration into global supply chains and boost manufacturing.
Evaluate the potential impact on India's 'Make in India' initiative and export competitiveness.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, and India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.

GS2.headline: Government Releases Draft National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Progress Report
GS2.explainer:
• Ministry of Education publishes a comprehensive report on the progress of NEP 2020 implementation.
• Highlights advancements in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and digital education initiatives.
• Identifies key challenges in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy.
• Invites public feedback on the report to refine future implementation strategies.
• Underscores the long-term vision of transforming India's education landscape for a knowledge economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
GS2.source: Ministry of Education / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020.
Major recommendations of NEP (e.g., 5+3+3+4 structure, multidisciplinary approach).
Objectives of Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) Mission.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
Key bodies involved in NEP implementation (NCERT, UGC, AICTE).
mainsPoints: Assess the progress and challenges in the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020 since its inception.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of equity, access, and quality in Indian education.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the transformational goals of NEP.
Suggest further measures to accelerate the successful implementation of NEP across all levels of education.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing the future of education.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Discuss Global Economic Stability and Debt Management at Year-End Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors convene for their final meeting of the year.
• Deliberations focus on addressing rising global debt vulnerabilities and inflationary pressures.
• Emphasize coordinated efforts to foster sustainable and inclusive economic growth worldwide.
• India reiterates its call for reforms in multilateral development banks to address developing nations' needs.
• Joint statement highlights commitment to financial stability and resilience against future shocks.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Mandate of G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Role of G20 in global economic governance.
Current global economic challenges discussed.
India's stance on MDB reforms.
Concept of sustainable and inclusive growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the G20 in addressing contemporary global economic challenges such as debt distress and inflation.
Discuss India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly concerning multilateral development bank reforms.
Examine the importance of international cooperation in achieving global financial stability and sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of G20 declarations in translating into concrete policy actions by member states.
GS2.imageDescription: Representatives from various countries seated around a conference table with G20 logo.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world's population, 80% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Urban Development Ministry Launches 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0' with Enhanced Focus on Sustainability and Inclusivity
GS2.explainer:
• Second phase of the flagship urban development program is unveiled.
• Expands scope to include more cities and integrates lessons from the first phase.
• Prioritizes green urban mobility, digital governance, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Aims to foster citizen-centric urban planning and improve quality of life.
• Emphasizes sustainable financial models and private sector participation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Smart Cities Mission.
Key features of 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0'.
Components of a smart city (e.g., smart waste management, smart transport).
Challenges of urbanization in India.
Role of technology in urban governance.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the achievements and shortcomings of the first phase of the Smart Cities Mission in India.
Discuss how 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0' addresses the challenges of rapid urbanization and promotes sustainable urban development.
Examine the importance of citizen participation and digital governance in creating truly 'smart' and livable cities.
Suggest innovative approaches for financing and implementing urban infrastructure projects under the mission.
GS2.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces and integrated technology, representing a smart city.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on 25 June 2015.

GS2.headline: India Strengthens Global Health Diplomacy with New Vaccine Initiatives and Collaborative Research
GS2.explainer:
• India expands its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative, providing assistance to developing nations.
• Announces new partnerships for vaccine research and development with international bodies.
• Focuses on equitable access to healthcare and pandemic preparedness globally.
• Highlights India's role as a reliable pharmaceutical and vaccine manufacturing hub.
• Aims to contribute significantly to global health security and universal health coverage.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Concept of Vaccine Maitri.
India's role as 'pharmacy of the world'.
Importance of vaccine equity.
International organizations involved in global health (WHO, GAVI).
India's contributions to COVAX.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing role in global health diplomacy and its contributions to addressing global health challenges.
Analyze the 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its impact on India's soft power and international relations.
Examine the challenges in ensuring equitable access to vaccines and essential medicines worldwide.
Evaluate the potential for India to become a global leader in pharmaceutical research and development, beyond manufacturing.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of the world with syringes and vaccine vials, symbolizing global health efforts.
GS2.trivia: India is the largest provider of generic drugs globally, meeting over 50% of vaccine demand worldwide.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Electoral Funding
GS2.explainer:
• Committee submits report suggesting greater transparency and accountability in political financing.
• Proposes caps on anonymous donations and real-time disclosure of contributions.
• Advocates for state funding of elections as a long-term measure to curb corruption.
• Recommends strengthening the Election Commission's powers to regulate political parties' finances.
• Aims to foster a level playing field and enhance democratic integrity.
gsSubTopics: Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
GS2.source: PRS Legislative Research / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: Current mechanisms for political funding in India (e.g., electoral bonds).
Arguments for and against state funding of elections.
Powers of Election Commission of India regarding political parties.
Recommendations for increasing transparency.
Role of the Representation of the People Act.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the existing framework of electoral funding in India and its impact on democratic processes.
Discuss the various recommendations made by the Parliamentary Standing Committee for comprehensive electoral finance reforms.
Examine the pros and cons of state funding of elections as a means to curb corruption and promote transparency.
Suggest measures to strengthen the Election Commission's authority in regulating political party finances and ensuring fair elections.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing the intersection of elections and money.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a permanent and independent body established by the Constitution of India.

GS3.headline: India's Economic Resilience Shines in 2024 Year-End Review, FY25 Growth Projected Strong
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of Finance releases year-end economic review highlighting robust growth.
• Key drivers include domestic demand, public capital expenditure, and manufacturing.
• Inflation remains within RBI's target band, though global uncertainties persist.
• Government reiterates commitment to fiscal consolidation path for FY25.
• India poised to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: India's projected GDP growth for FY25.
Key sectors contributing to growth (e.g., manufacturing, services, agriculture).
RBI's inflation target range.
Components of fiscal deficit (revenue deficit, capital expenditure).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India's economy in the coming fiscal year.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government's fiscal and monetary policies in maintaining stability and growth.
Examine the role of domestic demand and public investment in economic recovery and expansion.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic showing India's GDP growth trajectory and key economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: India aims to become a USD 5 trillion economy by 2027 and a USD 10 trillion economy by 2035.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieves a major milestone in RLV development.
• The test involved a precision landing and autonomous navigation demonstration.
• Aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches and enhance mission flexibility.
• RLV technology is crucial for future space exploration and commercial space missions.
• Positions India among a select few nations developing advanced reusable launch capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space research
prelimsPoints: Full form of RLV.
Objectives of RLV technology.
Benefits of reusable launch vehicles.
Other countries developing similar technology.
ISRO's previous RLV-TD missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its economic implications.
Analyze how RLV capabilities can enhance India's competitiveness in the global commercial space market.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for ISRO in developing fully operational RLVs.
Highlight the role of indigenization in India's space technology advancements.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) during re-entry.
GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD 'HEX' mission by ISRO in 2016 successfully demonstrated the hypersonic flight and autonomous landing technology.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Coastal Zone Projects, Mandates Stricter Safeguards
GS3.explainer:
• Apex court rules on a batch of petitions challenging coastal development projects.
• Affirms the necessity of balancing development with environmental protection.
• Directs a review mechanism for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) in ecologically sensitive areas.
• Emphasizes the need for robust public consultation and compensation frameworks.
• Ruling provides clarity for infrastructure projects in Coastal Regulation Zones (CRZ).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Purpose of CRZ Notification.
Role of EIA in environmental clearances.
Importance of public hearing in EIA process.
Different categories of CRZ areas.
Key principles of sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's approach to balancing developmental needs with environmental protection, particularly in coastal areas.
Discuss the effectiveness of the existing Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification and suggest potential reforms.
Examine the role of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) in ensuring sustainable infrastructure development.
Evaluate the implications of this ruling for future coastal development projects and local communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A developed coastal area with emphasis on both infrastructure and natural ecosystem.
GS3.trivia: The CRZ Notification was first issued in 1991 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, and has been amended several times since.

GS3.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 200 GW Mark, Accelerating Net-Zero Goals
GS3.explainer:
• India achieves a significant milestone in renewable energy, surpassing 200 GW installed capacity.
• This includes solar, wind, hydro, and biomass power sources.
• Reflects government's aggressive push towards cleaner energy and reduced carbon footprint.
• Boosts India's commitment to its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Paves the way for greater energy security and a green economy.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: India's total renewable energy capacity in GW.
Breakdown of capacity by source (solar, wind, hydro).
India's target for renewable energy by 2030.
Concept of NDCs under Paris Agreement.
Government schemes promoting renewable energy (e.g., PM-KUSUM).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's rapid growth in renewable energy capacity and its implications for climate change mitigation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and net-zero goals.
Examine the role of government policies, private investment, and technological advancements in the green energy transition.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of shifting towards a renewable energy-dominated grid.
GS3.imageDescription: Large solar panel farm stretching into the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy growth.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world, after China and the USA.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework to Combat Rising Digital Threats
GS3.explainer:
• Government unveils new measures to bolster national cyber defenses.
• Focuses on critical infrastructure protection and data privacy.
• Enhances cooperation between public and private sectors for threat intelligence sharing.
• Introduces updated guidelines for reporting cyber incidents and ensuring quick response.
• Aims to safeguard digital economy and citizen data from sophisticated cyber attacks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics & IT / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Role of CERT-In.
Key provisions of the IT Act, 2000 (as amended).
Importance of protecting critical information infrastructure.
Common types of cyber threats.
Concept of data localization.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of India's current cyber security framework and suggest measures for further strengthening it.
Examine the challenges in fostering public-private partnership for cyber defense and data protection.
Evaluate the balance between national security concerns and individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
GS3.imageDescription: Stylized digital lock icon overlaid with binary code, representing cyber security.
GS3.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.

GS3.headline: Agricultural Sector Registers Record Foodgrain Production in 2024, Focus Shifts to Crop Diversification
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of Agriculture releases final estimates for Kharif and Rabi seasons, showing record output.
• Attributed to favorable monsoon, government support, and improved farming practices.
• Government now emphasizes crop diversification to reduce reliance on water-intensive crops.
• Promotes pulses, oilseeds, and high-value horticulture to enhance farmer incomes.
• Addresses concerns over groundwater depletion and nutritional security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
GS3.source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / Livemint
prelimsPoints: Major foodgrain crops in India.
Difference between Kharif and Rabi crops.
Importance of monsoon for Indian agriculture.
Benefits of crop diversification.
Government initiatives for farmer welfare (e.g., PM-KISAN, FPOs).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to record foodgrain production in India and its implications for food security.
Discuss the necessity and challenges of crop diversification in the context of climate change and water scarcity.
Examine the government's role in promoting sustainable agricultural practices and enhancing farmer incomes.
Evaluate the impact of agricultural policies on cropping patterns and environmental sustainability.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green agricultural fields under a clear sky, symbolizing bountiful harvest.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice, wheat, and several other major foodgrains.
